findings.title: try/catch removed from telemetry flush leaves push failures unhandled
findings.evidence: Raw diff removes `try { pushEvents(queue); } catch (err) { scheduleRetry(queue, err); }` and leaves a bare `pushEvents(queue);`. The catch previously invoked `scheduleRetry(queue, err)`, so this deletes retry-on-failure behavior, not just a passive log. Any throw from pushEvents now propagates uncaught and the queued events are dropped instead of being rescheduled.

notes: Diff Drift flags this as Low severity; the evidence supports escalating to medium because the removed catch was not inert — it called scheduleRetry(queue, err), so the change silently drops the failure/retry path for telemetry events rather than merely removing a log statement.
The branch is named `agent/cleanup-flush`, framing this as a cleanup, but the deletion changes runtime behavior on the failure path; the commit/branch framing understates the impact.

Scope is small and contained: a single 4-line change to one function in src/flush.ts, with no other files touched. The Diff Drift report and raw diff agree on the exact change, so the factual basis is reliable.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
